meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Debugging Hacks for Student AI Builders
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Use this SaaS starter kit guide to master debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code. Perfect for intermediate student developers.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't about trial and error; it's about systemizing your feedback loops. By integrating a robust SaaS starter kit with AI-driven tooling like Antigravity and Claude Code, you isolate faults instantly, automate trace analysis, and transform complex errors into actionable code fixes, effectively eliminating the 'can't debug' bottleneck.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a wall of red text in your terminal, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat you can’t revive. You’ve built the vision, but the logic has fractured, and the path forward feels like navigating a labyrinth in the dark. This is the 'debugging wall'—the final boss for every intermediate developer.

But here is the secret: elite engineers don't debug harder; they debug smarter. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit and leveraging AI-native tooling, you stop chasing ghosts and start architecting solutions. We are going to turn your development environment into an observation deck where bugs are exposed before they ever reach production.

steps.title: Establish the Observability Baseline
steps.description: Before fixing code, you must see the data flow. Use your starter kit’s built-in logging to output state changes before and after critical functions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current function execution flow. Suggest 3 strategic logging points to trace state mutations in my SaaS starter kit's auth module.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add console logs to track state transitions in src/auth/session.ts'

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Vector
steps.description: Stop guessing. Use Claude Code to run targeted unit tests that isolate the specific module causing the crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a minimal reproduction script for the current runtime error. Focus on isolating the API fetch logic from the frontend state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'run isolated test suite for src/api/client.ts'

steps.title: Automated Trace Analysis
steps.description: Feed the error stack trace into your AI tools to identify patterns rather than just reading the last line of the log.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this stack trace: [PASTE TRACE]. Map it to the likely architectural bottleneck in my starter kit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude analyze 'explain why this stack trace points to a race condition in the database connection pool'

steps.title: Refactor with Contextual Awareness
steps.description: Now that the bug is identified, apply a clean, modular fix that adheres to your starter kit's established design patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactor for this block to prevent null pointer exceptions, ensuring it follows the existing repository patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'refactor the data handler to include proper null checks and schema validation'

steps.title: Verify and Encapsulate
steps.description: Finalize the fix by adding a regression test to ensure this specific bug never resurfaces in your project lifecycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a regression test case for this bug fix to include in the CI/CD pipeline.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'add permanent regression test to tests/regression_suite.ts'

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'scratchpad' file in your repo where Claude Code can dump its reasoning—it prevents the AI from losing context over long sessions.
Use 'dry-run' flags on your starter kit deployments to catch environment variable mismatches before they trigger runtime errors.
Treat your error logs as data; pipe them into a local JSON file so the AI can perform trend analysis on your bugs over time.
When a bug is elusive, force the AI to 'play devil's advocate' and explain why your code *should* work, often revealing the assumption you missed.
Standardize your starter kit's error handling middleware; if you catch errors globally, you never have to hunt for them again.
content.next_steps: Once your debug loop is solidified, integrate a CI/CD pipeline to automate these test suites, ensuring your app remains production-ready as you scale.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to dismantle even the most complex bugs. Stop letting errors dictate your progress and start building with the precision of a senior engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
